Here in Australia back in the early 1990’s Ross River Fever ( mosquito’s transition of diesease ) was rampant. Luckily a friends husband had gone to a special viral doctor who diagnosed me with Epstein- Barr  & Cytomegalovirus. I was bed ridden for 3 months and had a constant ringing in the ears which left me with an inner ear imbalance for a few months as well. There were many different vitamin muscle injections and other large amounts of vitamins including vitamin C which had to be taken orally or by muscular injection constantly for at least 2 years before there was any easing of the condition. I lived in Brisbane, Australia and not any exotic or highly polluted country. Many doctors did say it was in my head. Before a new outbreak came on I ate a huge amount of cornflakes and milk  which my body demanded, then the condition sent me back to bed for months, then struggled to keep awake and function, so I put on weight which increased my lethargy. At some point in my life I found a different normal and very slowly regained 50% of my original energy, if I remained on a mainly vegan diet and vitamin supplement routine with twice the dose of magnesium and joint help additional supplements. The krill oil capsules worked very well. Slowly my life was adjusting to this new condition called ‘Chronic Fatigue’ within the medical industry. Still considered to be a bit of a joke, but not for the person living with it.  40 years later it is still present. At least the ringing in the ears is not affecting my hearing abilities and I take a walk when I feel like it, but never overdo it. I keep to a light diet and have removed meat, meat by products. Dairy and by products, no alcohol, minimum of 4  litres of water per day and now add collagen with my other vitamins as bone broth is out. I eat sourdough bread with extra virgin olive oil and some other veg from my organic raised bed garden, and no nightshade family of fruit and veg. An alkaline diet to keep my ovarian cancer levels down to a very low blood count. My knees and feet are not good but the hospital will do nothing to fix them from a bad fall.  These things we live with. The pain is there daily but up I get and keep moving. I am also epileptic since birth and have PTSD.  This is my life. I prefer to live on my own by choice. It is much easier to keep my house clean and tidy this way, with my fur family.  I wish everyone who has this exhausting condition, a way to love yourself and care for yourself first. Then you are able to help others.  I went on to a disability pension after I was assaulted at work while cleaning, then had the Ovarian cancer shortly after the assaults which lead to the PTSD and the permanent disability pension. Money is very tight, but at least I have a house which still has a mortgage to pay, rates and Insurance costs.  I do like who I am now. It has taken many hard years to get to this place of inner peace, but it is possible. ♥️😁👍🏼🤗

This is a really great video and definitely something anyone should watch and understand if they’re considering trying keto or sugar fasting.  In the video, Nick Norwitz explains that both high-carb, low-protein (sugar fasting) and high-fat, low-protein (keto) diets elevate FGF21, which is the hormone responsible for increasing fat metabolism by raising energy expenditure. He also notes that genetic variants in FGF21 are extremely common—present in about 44% of people. If you have one of these variants, your FGF21 levels may not increase as expected on either diet, which means you might not experience the same rise in energy expenditure—and could even gain weight.  So why does he only offer caution about the sugar diet? That seems incredibly biased. I believe the same level of caution should be applied to keto as well, especially since, by his own explanation, the mechanism behind weight loss and increased energy expenditure is the same—FGF21. He also mentions that obesity is a state in which FGF21 resistance is likely. So, if you're obese and part of the 44% with a genetic variant, it’s very possible that neither of these diets will work as intended. Based on his interpretation of the study, the warning he gives about the sugar diet should absolutely apply to keto too.

The biggest problem in the food & beverage industry are the loopholes of "processing aids" "flavour carriers" etc. As a formulation chemist who works in this area its almost impossible to put volatile aromatic compounds - essential oils, terpenes etc into a water based environment due to the physical chemistry reality of oils & water dont mix. We have to use emulsifiers, surfactants, alcohols, or in this case various sugars such as maltodextrin to act as carriers in products. Marketing claims put forward by people with no real understanding of the chemistry or science involved make our jobs that much harder. Ive had clients turn around after Ive solved a separation issues in beverages to then say "We dont want any chemicals in our drink" - not realising that EVERYTHING in the Bloody drink IS chemicals!!! - Honesty & clear accuracy on labels mandated by laws internationally would stop the BS marketing nonsense & allow consumers to choose, alas those same consumers need to be educated to be able to make informed choices and many today are not. Thanks for this very balanced video on this issue & I agree the small amount of carbs in this electrolyte product shouldnt be of any metabolic concern for most people compared to a can of soda say. The real issue is that NO SUGAR claim on the label - that's deceptive or fraudulent advertising & here in Australia that would get your product cancelled by the TGA & a hefty fine issued for this infringement.
